description: Fix two issues found during fresh-user CLI testing of v0.0.38: (1) HIGH: `opentabs stop` says 'No OpenTabs server found' even when the server IS running — a regression from the health validation fix. The stop command doesn't send the auth header, so the health endpoint returns minimal `{"status":"ok"}` without the `version` field, and the new validation rejects it. (2) LOW: `config show` displays `browserToolPolicy` in a different position when offline (appended at end) vs online (between `tools` and `permissions`), because the normalization adds missing keys at the end of the object.

userStories.title: Fix `opentabs stop` to send auth header when checking health endpoint
userStories.description: As a user trying to stop a running OpenTabs server, I want `opentabs stop` to correctly detect the running server instead of saying 'No OpenTabs server found', so that the command works as expected.
acceptanceCriteria: `opentabs stop` sends the Authorization header (read from auth.json) when fetching the /health endpoint, matching the behavior of `opentabs status`
When the server is running (started without --background), `opentabs stop` prints a message acknowledging the server is running and advising how to stop it — NOT 'No OpenTabs server found'
When no server is running, `opentabs stop` still correctly reports 'Server is not running'
When a non-OpenTabs server is on the port, `opentabs stop` correctly reports 'No OpenTabs server found' (the validation still works for its intended purpose)

userStories.title: Normalize config show key ordering for consistent output
userStories.description: As a user running `opentabs config show`, I want the sections to appear in the same order whether the server is running or stopped, so that the output is predictable.
acceptanceCriteria: `opentabs config show` displays config sections in a consistent order: localPlugins, tools, browserToolPolicy, permissions — regardless of whether the key existed in the config file or was added by normalization
The order matches between server-running and server-stopped states
The JSON output (`--json`) also uses consistent key ordering
